origin: td-initial inline probe (Discovery already gathered evidence in ledger IP1; this artifact makes the citations addressable for assertion citations_pointer fields)
topic: GitHub API authentication, rate limits, and the specific objects required for pinned repos and contribution calendar

citations.claim: All GraphQL API requests must be authenticated. 'You must use a personal access token, OAuth token, GitHub App installation access token, or GitHub Actions token to authenticate.' Authorization header form: 'Authorization: bearer YOUR-TOKEN'.

ContributionCalendar exposes weeks[].contributionDays[].{date, contributionCount, color, weekday} and totalContributions for a contributionsCollection. A contributionsCollection accepts from/to ISO-8601 datetime arguments to scope the window (max 1 year per query).
applies_to: current-streak derivation from per-day contributionCount series

citations.claim: User.pinnedItems(first: N, types: [REPOSITORY]) returns up to 6 pinned items. Each Repository node exposes name, description, primaryLanguage{name,color}, stargazerCount, forkCount, url, isFork, isPrivate.

Unauthenticated REST requests are limited to 60 per hour per IP. Authenticated requests (PAT) are limited to 5,000 per hour. GraphQL rate limit is point-based (5,000 points/hour authenticated, no unauth quota).
applies_to: IP1 default branch — PAT path is required for any practical use

citations.claim: GET https://api.github.com/users/{username} returns 200 with the public user object (login, name, avatar_url, bio, html_url, public_repos, followers, following) or 404 if the username does not exist. CORS is enabled for browser origins.

GET https://api.github.com/users/{username}/repos?per_page=100&type=owner returns the user's owned public repositories. Each repo exposes a 'language' (primary language string) and 'languages_url'. The full per-language byte breakdown requires GET /repos/{owner}/{repo}/languages per repo.

The REST API supports CORS for AJAX requests from any origin (Access-Control-Allow-Origin: *). The GraphQL endpoint at https://api.github.com/graphql also returns permissive CORS headers for browser-origin POST requests with Authorization header.
applies_to: A9 — direct browser-to-api.github.com calls with no proxy

decisions_supported.decision: Use GraphQL for pinnedItems + contributionsCollection in a single query (one network round-trip for the two PAT-requiring data sources).
Use REST /users/{login} for basic user info (works unauthenticated within 60/hr limit and via the same PAT for higher limit).
Compute most-used language from REST /users/{login}/repos (page 1, per_page=100, type=owner, excluding forks) + per-repo /languages call for each non-fork owned repo. Bounded by 5000/hr PAT quota.
Issue the contributionsCollection query with from = (today - 89 days at 00:00 UTC), to = (today at 23:59 UTC) to get a 90-day window with full daily resolution.
Derive 'current streak' client-side from contributionCalendar.weeks[].contributionDays[] across a 1-year window (one extra query) to capture streaks longer than 90 days.
